other_observers: Heather Morris, Riley Morris, Rich Donley, John Bregar, Jim Beatty.; Heather Morris was the first one to see the swan. Riley Morris and I saw the bird within a very short time (about a minute later).  Independent observers (01/02/2008): Peter Derven, Barb Byron
duration: Observed the bird for about 30 minutes during above time.
description: The bird was obviously a swan because it was entirely white, very large (larger than a goose), had a long neck and black bill. The swan had yellow lores. The neck was slightly heavier and shorter than the neck of a Trumpeter Swan. The black facial skin formed a high, slightly curved arc on the forehead. The bill was black. The slope of the bill was steepest at the base of the culmen. The slope of the remainder of the culmen was more shallow.Behaviors: The swam was swimming on the pond and used feet to stir up food source.Call: No vocalizations heard.Plumage: Adult
